title: Benzilic Acid Rearrangement – Importance and Mechanism
section_name: VII. Named Organic Reactions in Alphabetical Order
summary: Overview page for the benzilic acid rearrangement describing base-induced rearrangement of alpha-diketones into alpha-hydroxy carboxylic acid salts through nucleophilic addition, 1,2-shift, and proton-transfer steps. The page also highlights ring-contraction variants and retro-benzilic acid rearrangements in cyclic systems.
notes: Emphasizes hydroxide or alkoxide attack on diketones, irreversible migration, and formation of alpha-hydroxy acid products from acyclic or cyclic alpha-diketones.

title: Benzilic Acid Rearrangement – Synthetic Applications
section_name: VII. Named Organic Reactions in Alphabetical Order
summary: Application page for the benzilic acid rearrangement featuring ring-contraction of a pyranosylated indolocarbazole toward the carbohydrate moiety of (+)-K252a, steroidal ketolactol rearrangement to a novel 5-ring D-homosteroid, retro-benzilic acid access to hinesol and agarospirol, and rearrangement routes to shinjulactone derivatives.
notes: Applications stress ring contraction, skeletal editing, and base-controlled migration in complex natural-product frameworks.

title: Benzoin and Retro-Benzoin Condensation – Importance and Mechanism
section_name: VII. Named Organic Reactions in Alphabetical Order
summary: Overview page for the benzoin and retro-benzoin condensation describing cyanide- or thiazolium-catalyzed umpolung coupling of aldehydes to alpha-hydroxy ketones and the reverse fragmentation of benzoins back to carbonyl compounds. The mechanism highlights cyanohydrin-type activation, Breslow-type thiazolium intermediates, proton transfer, and catalyst turnover.
notes: The page frames classical cyanide catalysis and modern thiazolium/NHC catalysis as complementary benzoin manifolds, with retro-benzoin cleavage as a useful ketone-forming strategy.

title: Benzoin and Retro-Benzoin Condensation – Synthetic Applications
section_name: VII. Named Organic Reactions in Alphabetical Order
summary: Application page for the benzoin and retro-benzoin condensation featuring ketone synthesis via cleavage of alpha-benzyloins, access to 2-substituted quinazolines, catalytic crossed aldehyde-ketone benzoin condensation to functionalized preanthraquinones, and benzoin-based assembly of diarylimidazoles.
notes: Applications show both constructive C-C bond formation and retro-benzoin fragmentation logic in heteroaromatic and medicinally relevant settings.

title: Bergman Cycloaromatization Reaction – Importance and Mechanism
section_name: VII. Named Organic Reactions in Alphabetical Order
summary: Overview page for the Bergman cycloaromatization reaction describing thermal or metal-assisted cycloaromatization of enediynes to benzenoid diradicals that abstract hydrogen atoms from donors such as cyclohexadiene. The page emphasizes distance and geometry requirements of the enediyne and the relevance of the resulting diradical to DNA-cleaving natural products.
notes: Focuses on enediyne-to-aromatic conversion, benzenoid diradical formation, and conditions such as 150-200 °C or Mg2+-induced cyclization.

title: Bergman Cycloaromatization Reaction – Synthetic Applications
section_name: VII. Named Organic Reactions in Alphabetical Order
summary: Application page for the Bergman cycloaromatization reaction featuring 10-membered pyrimidine enediynes, [n]phenacenoporphyrins, calicheamicin/speramicin-inspired trisulfide-triggered cyclizations, 7-methyl-2-methylene-acenaphthen-1-ol, and 2-tert-butylperylene.
notes: Applications highlight triggerable enediyne activation and rapid fused-ring construction from the initially formed diradical.

title: Biginelli Reaction – Importance and Mechanism
section_name: VII. Named Organic Reactions in Alphabetical Order
summary: Overview page for the Biginelli reaction describing acid-catalyzed three-component condensation of an aldehyde, a beta-keto ester, and urea or thiourea to give dihydropyrimidinones or related heterocycles. The mechanism features N-acyliminium-ion formation, enolized beta-keto ester attack, and cyclodehydration.
notes: The page collects classical and modified Biginelli conditions, including Atwal and Shuvalov-type variants, and emphasizes DHPM synthesis.

title: Biginelli Reaction – Synthetic Applications
section_name: VII. Named Organic Reactions in Alphabetical Order
summary: Application page for the Biginelli reaction featuring total syntheses of isocrambescidin 800, monastrol, and batzelladine F through tethered or intermolecular multicomponent condensation strategies.
notes: Applications show enantioselective/tethered Biginelli manifolds and medicinally relevant DHPM scaffolds.

title: Birch Reduction – Importance and Mechanism
section_name: VII. Named Organic Reactions in Alphabetical Order
summary: Overview page for the Birch reduction describing dissolving-metal reduction of aromatic rings or electron-poor heteroarenes with alkali metals in liquid ammonia and an alcohol proton source to give unconjugated cyclohexadienes or partially reduced heterocycles. The mechanism proceeds through sequential electron transfer, radical anion formation, protonation, and regiocontrolled second electron/proton delivery.
notes: Highlights substituent-dependent regioselectivity, use of Li or Na in NH3(l), and applicability to pyridines, furans, and related systems under electron-withdrawing substitution.

title: Birch Reduction – Synthetic Applications
section_name: VII. Named Organic Reactions in Alphabetical Order
summary: Application page for the Birch reduction featuring syntheses of (-)-taxol, (+)-apovincamine, galbulimima alkaloid GB 13, substituted 2-pyrrolines, and (+)-nemorensic acid through regioselective arene or heteroarene reduction and Birch reductive alkylation.
notes: Applications demonstrate Birch reductive alkylation, oxygenation after partial reduction, and strategic dearomatization in complex natural-product settings.
